original: NASA launches 128 baby squid and 5,000 microscopic animals, to study effects of spaceflight, to the International Space Station aboard Space X's Falcon 9 rocket
Innovative and much loved children's picture book "The Very Hungry Caterpillar" byEric Carleis published
1st US Space walk made by NASA astronaut Ed White during the Gemini 4 mission (23 minutes)
Rock climber Alex Honnold makes first-ever free solo climb of El Capitan, Yosemite National Park (depicted in "Free Solo" Academy Award-winning Best Documentary 2018)
The Amazing World ofDr. SeussMuseum opens in Springfield, Massachusetts
First artificial gene is synthesized
Dead whale found with 17 pounds (80 pieces) of plastic in its stomach in Songkhla province, Thailand
Josh Gibsonhits a home run just 2 feet below the rim of Yankee Stadium (580' drive)
Korczak Ziolkowski begins sculpture of Crazy Horse near Mt Rushmore
Dr. Jesse Selber performs the world's first partial-skull and scalp transplant at Houston Methodist Hospital
Queen's "Bohemian Rhapsody" goes gold
Last episode of Star Trek airs on NBC (Turnabout Intruder)
Future Baseball Hall of Fame first basemanLou Gehrigfirst to hit 4 consecutive HRs in a MLB game; NY Yankees beat Philadelphia A's, 20-13 at Shibe Park
Baseball poem "Casey at the Bat" is first published by the San Francisco Examiner
200" (5.08 m) Hale telescope dedicated at Palomar Observatory
Mount Unzen erupts in Japan, worst eruption in Japanese history
NASA administrator Bill Nelson announces two new missions to study ‘Lost Habitable’ world of Venus in 2028 and 2030
Brewers draft B J Surhoff #1
1st African American to graduate from US Naval Academy (Wesley Anthony Brown)
Goodyear's first advertising airship, later named "Pilgrim" takes flight over Akron, Ohio; it is the first to fly using helium, and first to have an enclosed cabin
French expedition reaches top of Himalayan peak of Annapurna in Nepal
New Zealand teenage singer-songwriterLordereleases her debut single "Royals" (Grammy Song of the Year, 2014)

Island Records releases "Exodus", the ninth studio album byBob Marley& the Wailers; featuring the hits, 'Jamming', 'Waiting In Vain', 'Three Little Birds' and 'One Love', it is named the best album of the century by Time Magazine in 1999
John Dykstra, American special effects artist and winner of 3 Oscars (Star Wars, Spider-Man 2), born in Long Beach, California[1]
Ray Daviesof The Kinks travels round trip NY-London to change 1 word in "Lola," (Coca-Cola to Cherry Cola) because of BBC commercial reference ban
"Big," a film directed byPenny Marshalland starringTom Hanks, premieres in the US
Melissa Mathison, American screenwriter (E.T.), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2015)
MLB Chicago Cubs pitcherKen Holtzman2nd no-hitter beats Cincinnati Reds, 1-0, on the road at Riverfront Stadium
Jack Jouett rides to warnThomas Jeffersonof British attack
ExplorerAlexander Mackenziesets out on his first expedition to the Pacific from Fort Chipewyan (finds the Arctic Ocean instead)
The Canadian Pacific Railway is completed from coast to coast
Houston Astros beat LA Dodgers, 5-4, in 22 innings (7:14:09)
US presented with oldest known copy of Magna Carta
Aretha Franklin's cover of theOtis Reddingsong "Respect" reaches #1
Gemini 4 launches; second US two-man flight (McDivitt and White); mission includes first US spacewalk
Gemini 9 launched; 7th US 2-man flight (Stafford & Cernan)
Crew of Soyuz 36,Valery Kubasovand Bertalan Farkas return to Earth aboard Soyuz 35
American PresidentJFKand Soviet PremierKhrushchevmeet in Vienna
Liverpool Football Club is founded
Edith McGuire, American athlete (Olympic gold women's 200m, silver 100m & 4x100m relay 1964), born in Atlanta, Georgia
Ibrahim Hussein, Kenyan marathonrunner(Boston Marathon-1988, 91, 92), born in Kapsabet, Kenya
Devon Conway becomes 7th batsman in cricket history to score a double century in his Test debut; run out for 200 for New Zealand in 1st Test against England at Lord's
Hashim Amla becomes the first non-white captain of the South African national cricket team
Thomas Hearnscaptures WBA light heavyweight title
Ringo Starrcollapses from tonsillitis and pharyngitis, just before the Beatles embark on a World Tour

"The Party at the Palace" concert commemorating Queen Elizabeth's Golden Jubilee held at Buckingham Palace Garden in London; performers include: Queen,Annie Lennox,Tony Bennett,Tom Jones; The Corrs;Ozzy Osbourne,Brian Wilson, andPaul McCartney, among others
"Little Shop of Horrors" released in France
World's largest environmental summit opens in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
Last British and French troops evacuated from Dunkirk
US & Cuba talk about diplomatic relations
Mexican architect Luis Barragán is awarded architecture's Pritzker Prize at a ceremony at Dumbarton Oaks, Washington D.C.
Apple announces it is shutting down iTunes and replacing it with three different apps
Largest-ever exhibition of works byPiet Mondrianopens at the Gemeentemuseum in The Hague, Netherlands to mark centenary of De Stijl movement
"MTV Unplugged" broadcasts singer-songwriterPaul Simonand 11 of his band members performing in Queens, NYC
"Hot Rod Lincoln" by Commander Cody & His Lost Planet Airmen hits #9
Comanche, Iowa, completely destroyed by one of a series of tornadoes
A sudden cloudburst kills 120 near Pikes Peak, Colorado
Supreme Liberty Life Insurance Co (Chicago) - first insurance company organized by African Americans formed
1st trade show at Atlantic City Convention Center (electric light)
One thousand unemployed Canadian workers board freight cars in Vancouver, British Columbia, beginning a protest trek to Ottawa, Ontario
